mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held near the zero lower bound (0.11%) signals an accommodative monetary policy stance that lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, producing systematically positive S&P 500 forward returns as long as the rate remains below 0.5%.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.11 as of t-1mo, near zero lower bound
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during periods with FEDFUNDS < 0.5% are not significantly higher than the unconditional sample mean (t-test p > 0.05), or rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate of at least 0.2 percentage points signals improving labor market conditions that support consumer spending and corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by >= 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: In triggered months, the proportion of positive 20-day forward returns does not exceed 55%, or the mean forward return in triggered periods is statistically indistinguishable from non-triggered periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment (PAYEMS) continues to expand while industrial production (INDPRO) growth stalls or contracts, the divergence signals a late-cycle mismatch between labor demand and real output that precedes equity market weakness over a 60-day horizon, as margin compression eventually forces payroll retrenchment.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth > 1% while INDPRO 3-month annualized growth < 0%
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following triggered divergence episodes are not significantly negative relative to baseline (p > 0.05), or rank IC is below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) accelerating above their trailing 12-month average signal strengthening residential investment demand that amplifies equity market gains specifically when monetary policy is accommodative, because low borrowing costs allow housing strength to translate more fully into broader consumption and construction employment.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average exceeds trailing 12-month average by more than 5%
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with near-zero policy rate
failure_condition: During periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.5% and the HOUST trigger fires, 60-day forward returns are not significantly greater than unconditional baseline returns (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month momentum in the Industrial Production Index (INDPRO) reflects expanding real economic output that supports near-term corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a short 5-day horizon as markets incrementally price in improved output data.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with trailing INDPRO momentum (rank IC < 0.02) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth exceeding 0.5% month-over-month signals robust consumer demand that supports corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following triggered RSAFS growth episodes fail to exceed the unconditional mean forward return at p > 0.05 significance.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) above a 2.5% year-over-year pace raises market expectations of imminent policy tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in months where the CPI trigger fires are not significantly negative relative to baseline (p > 0.05), or the effect reverses sign across two consecutive rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During an early-cycle recovery with near-zero policy rates, a widening gap between elevated unemployment (UNRATE) and low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals sustained policy accommodation relative to labor slack, jointly supporting risk asset appreciation; the interaction of a still-high UNRATE with persistently low FEDFUNDS predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days, an effect not captured by either series alone.
regime_hint: early-cycle recovery with near-zero policy rate and elevated slack
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during joint UNRATE>8%/FEDFUNDS<0.5% regimes are not significantly different from returns during periods when only one condition holds (p > 0.05), or rank IC of the joint signal is below the rank IC of either single-series signal.
